title: Opiniones
summary: Say what you think, and whether you agree.

teach.target: Io pensa que Interlingua es facile.
teach.roman: io pensa que interlingua es facile
teach.english: I think that Interlingua is easy.
teach.note: que introduces a clause, like English 'that' — Interlingua just uses the normal verb form here, no subjunctive.
teach.ipa: ˈio pˈensa kˈe ˌinterlˈiŋɡwa es fˈatsile

grammar.title: Saying what you think
grammar.body:
**Io pensa que...** / **Io crede que...** (I think/believe that...) work exactly like English — no special mood needed after que.
**Secundo me** (in my opinion) can open a sentence on its own: Secundo me, isto es un bon idea.

teach.target: Es possibile que ille ha ration.
teach.roman: es possibile que ille ha ration
teach.note: haber ration = to be right, literally 'to have reason' — the same haber-idiom as haber fame/haber sete.
teach.ipa: es posˈibile kˈe ˈile ha ratjˈon

canDo: Say what you think or believe
Say whether you agree or disagree
